info.title: Sly API (internal)
info.description: The Agentic Economy Platform — stablecoin payments, agent wallets, multi-protocol commerce, and AI agent orchestration.

post.description: Register an API endpoint that will accept x402 micropayments. Returns a payment address and asset details to advertise in 402 responses.

get.description: List all x402 endpoints registered by your tenant.

409.description: Endpoint has transaction history; add ?force=true to confirm

post.description: Issue a settlement token for a specified recipient and amount. The token is JWS-signed and contains the settlement instructions. Use `POST /settle` to execute.
Execute settlement against the rail specified in the token. Returns the settlement record with on-chain tx_hash or rail_reference.
Issue and settle a UCP token backed by an AP2 mandate — no pre-issued token needed. Mandate scope is verified on execution.
Get FX rate, fee, and estimated settlement time for a corridor. Expires in ~30 seconds. Use to display to user before settlement.

get.description: Enumerates active corridors for your tenant (e.g. USD→BRL via Pix, USD→MXN via SPEI), their limits, and typical settlement times.
Machine-readable capability advertisement — what your UCP installation supports. Usually consumed via `.well-known/ucp`.

post.description: Create many checkouts in one request — useful for bulk commerce (campaign rollouts, marketplace listings).
Complete payments for many checkouts at once — e.g. when a platform settles an end-of-day batch.

put.description: Full update — only allowed while status is `created` or `pending_payment`. Use PATCH /{id}/edit for partial changes.

delete.description: Only allowed before payment — soft delete with audit trail.

post.description: Execute payment. For card checkouts, pass `payment_method_id` from your Stripe/Adyen integration. For stablecoin checkouts, pass `from_wallet_id`.

patch.description: Modify subset of fields (items, metadata, expires_at). Allowed before payment completion.

post.description: Attach a payment method (card token, bank reference, wallet) to this checkout.
If the order is paid, a refund is automatically issued. Triggers `ucp.order.cancelled` webhook.
Declare what the merchant is committing to deliver — shipping ETA, digital delivery window, etc. Used by dispute resolution.
Merchant-side timeline entry (shipped / in_transit / delivered / etc.). Triggers webhooks and feeds dispute evidence.
Ad-hoc post-checkout change (discount applied, shipping fee adjusted, partial refund). Creates a ledger entry tied to the order.

get.description: Initiates the OAuth authorization code flow. Redirects user to consent screen.

302.description: Redirect to consent or back to redirect_uri with code

post.description: Exchange authorization code for access token, or refresh an existing token.
Called from the consent UI after the user approves the requested scopes. Returns the authorization code.
Register a merchant or third-party app that will link buyer accounts. Returns `client_secret` once.

get.description: Accounts the authenticated principal has linked via OAuth. Used to enumerate buyer relationships.

delete.description: Revokes all tokens associated with the linkage and removes it.

get.description: Discovery. Filter by category, country, or search text. Use for building merchant picker UI in agent-facing clients.
Full merchant detail including accepted rails, settlement prefs, and any custom metadata the merchant published.

post.description: Opens an Agentic Commerce Protocol session. Returns `client_secret` — use with Stripe.js / Stripe Elements to collect payment client-side.
Create up to 100 checkouts in one call. Useful for marketplace baskets where one agent purchase spans many merchants.

patch.description: Swap items, shipping address, or payment method before completion.

delete.description: Allowed only for un-paid checkouts. Soft-delete retains audit trail.

post.description: Execute payment. For card checkouts, pass `payment_method_id` (typically from Stripe Elements or SetupIntent). For stablecoin, pass `from_wallet_id`.

403.description: KYA tier or wallet policy blocked

get.description: Public AP2 capability advertisement — issuer, public key, supported scopes. Used by merchants to verify mandates offline.

post.description: Issue a signed mandate authorizing an agent to spend within a scope. Returns `mandate_jwt` — present on every execution.

patch.description: Modify caps or merchant rules in-place. Prior scope retained in audit log.
Soft-cancel — mandate goes to `cancelled`, audit trail preserved.

post.description: Resume a paused or suspended mandate.
Temporarily disable — no executions allowed until resumed.
Hard revoke — mandate state `revoked`, cannot be reactivated.
Each execution re-checks scope (amount within caps, merchant allowed, not expired) + day/month running totals + KYA tier + wallet policy. If any check fails, returns 403 with the specific reason.

403.description: Scope, policy, or KYA tier check failed

post.description: Low-level payment primitive used by mandate executions. Rarely called directly — use /mandates/:id/execute.

409.description: Already settled or in invalid state

get.description: Find agents offering a given skill, ranked by reputation, price, or recency. Public via the non-authed /a2a/marketplace mount; this authed version carries tenant context for personalized ranking.

post.description: Single M2M payment without opening a session. Useful for first-call or one-off payments.
Pre-deposit a budget for streaming micropayments to a service. Sign vouchers via /sessions/:id/voucher for each call. Close via /sessions/:id/close.
Sign a voucher for a unit of service. Budget decrements; remaining balance returned in response.
Finalize all signed vouchers, debit the agent wallet, return unspent deposit.
Server-sent event stream of voucher activity for a session. Useful for live dashboards during a long-running session.

200.description: SSE stream — each event includes voucher_id, amount, budget_remaining

post.description: Provision a Tempo wallet capable of voucher signing. One-time per agent.

get.description: Live-probe a service's MPP pricing endpoint (HEAD / well-known probe).

post.description: Service-side: verify a voucher claim against the issuing session.

budget_utilization.description: Average ratio of budget spent to deposited

post.description: Invoke an MCP method (tools/list, tools/call, resources/list, resources/read). Follows the Model Context Protocol JSON-RPC spec.
Creates a person or business account under the authenticated tenant. Accounts start at verification tier 0; use POST /v1/accounts/:id/verify to trigger KYC/KYB.

get.description: Paginated list. Filter by type (person / business), verification status, or free-text search over name + email.

patch.description: Partial update. Only provided fields change; omit a field to leave it as-is. Set `email: null` to clear an email.

delete.description: Soft-deletes the account. Fails if the account has agents, open streams, or a non-zero balance. Detach / drain those first.

post.description: Creates an account, an optional agent, and a wallet in a single atomic call. Returns all generated credentials. Useful for wizards and scripted setups. Sandbox-funded if `initial_funding` is provided.

Move funds between accounts. Same-currency transfers settle instantly; cross-currency transfers require a prior `quote_id` from POST /v1/quotes. Always send an `Idempotency-Key` header to safely retry.

schema.description: Prevents duplicate transfers on retry; cached for 24h

parameters.description: Prevents duplicate transfers on retry; cached for 24h
parameters.name: idempotency-key

201.description: Transfer created (may still be pending settlement)

403.description: KYA tier, wallet policy, or approval threshold blocked

409.description: Idempotency key reused with different parameters

get.description: Paginated, richly filterable.

post.description: Cancels a transfer that hasn't yet been submitted to the settlement rail. Once a transfer is in `processing` or `completed`, cancellation fails — use [/v1/refunds](#tag/Refunds) instead.

409.description: Invalid state transition — transfer already processing or completed

post.description: Register an AI actor under a parent account. The response contains credentials — the `agent_*` token and (if requested) an Ed25519 keypair. Both the token and the private key are shown **once** and not recoverable.

schema.description: Filter to agents currently holding an SSE session

parameters.description: Filter to agents currently holding an SSE session
parameters.name: connected

delete.description: Permanent revocation. All credentials (agent_*, sess_*, Ed25519 keys) are invalidated. Agent wallets are detached but not deleted — funds remain claimable.

get.description: Returns agent's KYA tier caps, the parent account's KYC tier caps, and the effective (minimum-of-both) limits. Plus spend-to-date against each.

post.description: Agent can still authenticate and read data; spending is blocked. Non-destructive — unfreeze via /activate. For full revocation use DELETE /agents/:id or DELETE /agents/:id/auth-keys.
Create a wallet under an account or agent. Network auto-selects based on currency + tenant config if not specified. Stablecoin wallets get an on-chain address; fiat-ledger wallets don't.
Register an on-chain address the customer already controls — Sly tracks it but does not custody funds. Useful for payouts to user-controlled wallets.

delete.description: Fails if the wallet has a non-zero balance. Drain first via /withdraw.

get.description: Point-in-time balance snapshot. `available` = spendable now; `pending` = held by in-flight transfers.

post.description: Creates an internal transfer from the specified source wallet to this wallet. Both wallets must be same currency.
Move funds out — to another Sly wallet (internal) or an on-chain address (external). For large amounts may trigger [compliance hold](/settlement/overview#degraded-modes).
Open a continuous payment flow. `initial_deposit` is locked into the stream escrow; flow_rate drains it per second. Returns the stream with computed `runway_seconds`.
Pauses a stream. Paused streams don't accrue balance but wrapped funds stay locked.
Resumes a stream. Paused streams don't accrue balance but wrapped funds stay locked.
Cancellation transfers accrued balance to recipient and returns unused funds to sender. Stream state becomes `cancelled`.
Add funds to extend runway. Sender wallet is debited; wrapped_balance increases.
Creates a `stream_withdraw` transfer moving accrued recipient_balance into the recipient wallet. Omit amount to withdraw all accrued.
Get a time-bound FX rate quotation for cross-currency transfers. Default TTL ~30 seconds. Use the returned `id` on POST /v1/transfers to lock the rate.

get.description: Informational rate snapshot across all your enabled corridors. NOT a quote — do not use for execution.

post.summary: Lock FX rate for extended window
post.description: Enterprise feature — lock a rate for minutes-to-hours at a higher spread. Returns a quote-like token usable on transfers within the lock window.
Quote a chained conversion (e.g. USD → USDC → BRL via Pix) in a single call. Useful for cross-rail transfers where direct corridors don't exist.

Initiate a refund against a completed transfer. Omit `amount` for full refund. Multiple partial refunds per transfer are supported as long as the cumulative total ≤ original amount. Always send `X-Idempotency-Key`.

409.description: Refund window expired, amount exceeds remaining, or state transition invalid

schema.description: Filter to disputes with response window expiring in <72h

parameters.description: Filter to disputes with response window expiring in <72h
parameters.name: due_soon

409.description: Dispute window expired or duplicate dispute

get.description: Includes full timeline from audit log.

post.description: The counterparty submits their side with counter-evidence. Must be within `response_window_ends_at`; missing the window auto-resolves in the filer's favor.

409.description: Response window expired or dispute already resolved

post.description: Terminal action. If `resolution` is `full_refund` or `partial_refund`, auto-creates a [refund](#tag/Refunds).

cancelMandate.description: If the dispute relates to an AP2 mandate, optionally revoke it

get.description: Counts by status, reason, resolution outcome, and average resolution time. Useful for product/ops dashboards.

post.description: Returns `secret` (whsec_*) shown once — save it to verify signatures. See [signature verification](/sdks/webhook-verification).

get.description: Returns the machine-readable catalog of event types your tenant can subscribe to.
Per-endpoint and tenant-wide delivery counters (delivered, failed, retrying, DLQ).

post.description: Replay events from a time window. Use `only_failed: true` to skip already-delivered events. Replays carry the same `X-Sly-Event-Id`; your dedupe protects against double-processing.

override_url.description: Redirect replays to a different URL — useful when testing a new endpoint against real traffic

get.description: Deliveries that exhausted all retries. Subscribe to `webhook.dlq` for real-time alerts.

delete.description: Permanently removes DLQ entries. Usually done after manual review confirms the events are either truly failed or have been processed manually.

post.description: Returns the plaintext key **once**. Save it to your secrets manager immediately — Sly stores only a hash. Use narrow scopes in production.

get.description: Returns metadata only (prefix is safe to display). The plaintext keys are never retrievable after creation.

delete.description: Permanent. The key is invalidated instantly; cached verifications may persist up to 60s. Use alongside [overlap rotation](/authentication/api-keys#rotate-a-key) for zero-downtime swaps.

post.description: Issues a new key with the same scopes and name, then invalidates the old one. Returns the new plaintext key **once**.
Card, bank, or crypto wallet that funds Sly accounts. Bank/card sources require verification before they can fund.

409.description: In-flight transactions still reference this source

post.description: For bank accounts, sends micro-deposits or initiates Plaid identity check. For cards, performs $0 auth. Status updates async via webhook.

409.description: Already verified or in invalid state

post.description: Returns a short-lived session token to pass to the Plaid Link or Belvo widget client-side. The user completes the link flow in the browser; the resulting public_token is then used with POST /sources.
Pulls funds from a verified source into a Sly wallet. Always include `idempotency_key` — duplicate funding is the most expensive bug class.

post.summary: Estimate fees for a funding request
post.description: Preview provider + Sly fees + FX spread before committing. Use to display costs to the end user.
Lock a rate for a from→to currency pair. Use the returned `id` on POST /transactions to use the quoted rate.
Returns a Sly-hosted URL where an end user can fund a wallet without your front-end handling card data. Single-use, expires in 1 hour.
Embedded Coinbase on-ramp. User completes funding in Coinbase's flow; wallet credited on confirmation.
Move funds out of a Sly wallet to a registered bank account via the Coinbase off-ramp.
Crossmint hosted card-to-USDC flow. Mobile- and web-friendly.
Show projected fees, FX, and settlement time for a hypothetical settlement before committing.
Propose the optimal rail + path for a settlement. Returns one or more route options ranked by cost / time.
Force-execute a settlement using the supplied transfer + route. Bypass auto-settlement for cases that need manual control.
Settle many pending transfers in one call. Mostly used by ops + auto-settlement workers.
Bypass all rules and trigger a settlement immediately. Used during incident recovery or month-end close.

get.description: Next N window firings across all configured rails.

post.description: Manually enqueue a transfer for the next matching window. Usually managed automatically.
Bypass batching and force-settle everything pending on the given rail. Use sparingly — sacrifices batching economics.
Add a tenant-specific holiday (e.g. internal company closure) that windows will skip.

get.summary: Is a date a rail holiday?

get.description: High-level operational view — recent firings, queue depth, upcoming schedule.
Includes per-discrepancy breakdown.

post.description: Trigger a recon for a specific rail + date range. Runs async; subscribe to `reconciliation.completed` for completion.

get.description: Authoritative balance per the rail provider. Compare to your Sly-side wallet balance for spot-check sanity.
Raw transactions as reported by the rail. Useful for matching against Sly ledger entries when investigating discrepancies.
Discrepancy counts + delta totals across all recent recons. Health overview.

stale_count.description: Discrepancies older than 3 days still unresolved

get.description: Comprehensive snapshot — balances by currency, exposure, alerts, recent activity.
Projects when current balances will be exhausted at recent burn rate.
For platforms — how much each end-customer (sub-tenant) holds.

post.description: Pulls authoritative balances from each rail and refreshes Sly's cached treasury view.
Stores a point-in-time treasury record for compliance / audit purposes.

patch.description: Adjust min/target/max balance limits. Used to retune rebalancing thresholds.

post.description: For non-customer-originated movements (e.g. wires from corporate bank). Creates a ledger entry tied to a treasury account.
Move funds between treasury accounts (e.g. excess operating → reserve).
Configure a recurring or future-dated transfer. Use `frequency: custom` with a cron expression for arbitrary cadences.
Skips executions until resumed. In-flight executions complete.
Resume after pause. Next execution lands on the next scheduled time.
Permanently cancels remaining executions. Schedule transitions to `cancelled` (irreversible).
Bypasses the schedule and triggers a one-off execution immediately. Counts toward `maxOccurrences` if set.

bearerAuth.description: API key (pk_test_* or pk_live_*), JWT session, agent token (agent_*), Ed25519 session (sess_*), or portal token (portal_*).

mandate_jwt.description: Ed25519-signed JWT verifiable offline via the JWKS endpoint

voucher_key.description: Ed25519 public key for voucher verification

signed_voucher.description: Signed voucher the service verifies to collect payment

initial_funding.description: Sandbox only — funds the wallet from the faucet

quoteId.description: Required for cross-currency transfers; lock FX rate via POST /v1/quotes first

connected.description: True iff an SSE session is currently open

privateKey.description: base64 Ed25519 private key — shown ONCE

generateKeypair.description: If true, returns an Ed25519 keypair (private key shown once)

network.description: Auto-selected based on currency + tenant config if omitted

flow_rate.description: Amount per second, as a decimal string

secret.description: whsec_* — shown ONCE on creation; used for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification

prefix.description: First 12 chars of the key — safe to display

network.description: For crypto_wallet — base, polygon, solana, etc.
